_metadata.description: Reviewer-signed per-product canonical_id + IQM form overrides for DSLD rows where the row text alone is ambiguous but the surrounding product-name context disambiguates the right IQM/other_ingredients parent and form.
_metadata.purpose: Per-product canonical_id rerouting with provenance. Each entry rewrites a single (dsld_id, raw_ingredient_text) pair to a corrected canonical_id + explicit IQM form choice. The override is scoped to the specific dsld_id - global aliasing of the row text is explicitly NOT supported because it would false-match the same text on unrelated products.
_metadata.scope: Fires only for an exact (dsld_id, raw_ingredient_text) tuple match (case-insensitive, whitespace-stripped). product_name_must_contain_any is an additional safety guard: even if the (dsld_id, raw_text) tuple matches, the override only applies if product_name actually contains one of the listed substrings - defends against a DSLD ID being reassigned to a different product post-shipment.
_metadata.review_policy: Every entry must carry: id, dsld_id, brand, product_name, product_name_must_contain_any, raw_ingredient_text, set_canonical_id, set_canonical_source_db, EITHER set_preferred_iqm_form (exact form key) OR set_match_text_for_iqm (string injected as IQM match input), rationale, evidence_pmid_or_url (PubMed-verified PMID required for any new citation - run scripts/api_audit/verify_pubmed_references.py before commit), reviewer (must be a named team or individual, never a placeholder like "(pending)", "TODO", "TBD"), clinical_review_status (e.g., "identity_routing_reviewed"), review_scope (e.g., "context_identity_routing_only"), review_date.
_metadata.verification_policy: After commit, the live corpus rerun MUST confirm: (a) each listed dsld_id now emits the override canonical_id AND override form; (b) bio_score on the scored row matches the chosen IQM form (not the unspecified fallback); (c) the override fires for that dsld_id only; (d) scoring moves from NOT_SCORED to a scored verdict.
_metadata.fail_safe_policy: If set_preferred_iqm_form does not resolve under set_canonical_id in ingredient_quality_map.json, the enricher must NOT silently succeed with the unspecified form. Either error out or fall back to normal matching with a clear log warning. The test_set_preferred_iqm_form_resolves_in_iqm pytest guard runs at commit time to catch typos before they ship.
_metadata.loaded_by: scripts/enhanced_normalizer.py via _load_context_canonical_overrides (cached on first product); consumed by scripts/enrich_supplements_v3.py before _match_quality_map / _match_multi_form pipeline.

265081.rationale: Jarrow Type II Bioavailable Collagen Complex is BioCell hydrolyzed chicken sternal cartilage extract (MW ~5,500 Da; BioCell Tech patents US 6,780,841 / US 8,563,045). The row text 'Chicken Sternum Collagen extract' alone is ambiguous (could be undenatured UC-II at MW ~600,000 Da). Product name 'Type II Bioavailable' is the BioCell brand framing - UC-II would say 'Undenatured Type II'. Force the hydrolyzed form because the matcher cannot infer it from row text alone.
265081.evidence_pmid_or_url: https://www.biocelltechnology.com (BioCell Collagen manufacturer; brand provenance for hydrolyzed chicken sternal cartilage MW ~5,500 Da)

317962.rationale: Pure Encapsulations DAO Enzyme uses purified porcine kidney protein extract as the source of histamine-degrading DAO enzyme activity (10,000 HDU per 4.2 mg dose - industry canonical). The row text 'Porcine Kidney Extract' alone is ambiguous (used in legitimate kidney glandulars for adrenal/urinary support, not DAO activity). Product name 'DAO Enzyme' + the 4.2 mg dose disambiguate to diamine_oxidase IQM parent. Single-form parent; form selection is low-risk.
317962.evidence_pmid_or_url: PMID 31807350 (Diamine oxidase supplementation improves symptoms in patients with histamine intolerance - content-verified via PubMed API 2026-05-24)

259304.rationale: Natures Way Barley Grass / Barley Grass Powder uses dehydrated young barley grass (Hordeum vulgare leaf) - the bio_score, chlorophyll/SOD content, and nutritional profile of the grass differ from malt barley, barley seed flour, or beta-glucan extracts (from barley seed). The row text 'Barley' alone is ambiguous; the product name 'Barley Grass' is the safe disambiguator. Single-form parent; explicit form choice for consistency with other overrides.
259304.evidence_pmid_or_url: barley_grass IQM parent already exists; no new PMID required

259306.rationale: See 259304 - same Natures Way product line, powder format.
259306.evidence_pmid_or_url: barley_grass IQM parent already exists; no new PMID required
